perturbation.original_text: A. Contractor agrees to be compensated by the Company as follows: i. Fee The Company will pay Contractor $150,000 annually for the first full year to be adjusted proportionately with increases commensurate and at the same time with those awarded to the CEO and COO of the Company during the term of this contract. ii. For the first year only a Payment of $25,000 of the Fee shall be advanced to Contractor within the first six months of this Agreement. Additionally, the Contractor will be paid a fee of $10,416.66 per month over the next 12 months. Allowing the contractor to be paid the above agreed upon amount of $150,000 for first twelve months
perturbation.changed_text: A. Contractor agrees to be compensated by the Company as follows: i. Fee The Company will pay Contractor $150,000 annually for the first full year to be adjusted proportionately with increases commensurate and at the same time with those awarded to the CEO and COO of the Company during the term of this contract. ii. For the first year only a Payment of $50,000 of the Fee shall be advanced to Contractor within the first six months of this Agreement. Additionally, the Contractor will be paid a fee of $10,416.66 per month over the next 12 months. Allowing the contractor to be paid the above agreed upon amount of $150,000 for first twelve months
perturbation.explanation: The change increases the upfront payment from $25,000 to $50,000 within the first six months, contradicting the later monthly payment schedule which, when added to the increased upfront payment, will exceed the agreed-upon $150,000 annual payment amount. This creates an in-text contradiction, because now is unclear how much the contractor will be paid on total.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ii. For the first year only a Payment of $50,000 of the Fee shall be advanced to Contractor within the first six months of this Agreement. Additionally, the Contractor will be paid a fee of $10,416.66 per month over the next 12 months. Allowing the contractor to be paid the above agreed upon amount of $150,000 for first twelve months
perturbation.justification: The changed text increases the upfront payment from $25,000 to $50,000. The contradicted text states that 'Contractor will be paid a fee of $10,416.66 per month over the next 12 months. Allowing the contractor to be paid the above agreed upon amount of $150,000 for first twelve months'. If the contractor is paid $50,000 upfront and $10,416.66 per month for 12 months, the total payment would be $50,000 + ($10,416.66 * 12) = $175,000. This exceeds the stated 'agreed upon amount of $150,000', thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: D. Termination of Work and Deliverables . Contractor agrees that, upon termination of our contract with the Company (voluntary or otherwise), Contractor will return to the Company all things belonging to the Company, and that all documents, records, notebooks and tangible articles containing or embodying confidential information, including copies thereof, then in our possession or control, whether prepared by Contractor or others, will be left with the Company. Deliverables shall also included, but not be limited to: (i) all source code; (ii) firmware; (iii) executable code; (iv) assembled code; (v) documentation including design and any modification thereto, (vi) schematics; (vii) prototype and/or production hardware.
perturbation.changed_text: D. Termination of Work and Deliverables . Contractor agrees that, upon termination of our contract with the Company (voluntary or otherwise), Contractor will retain a copy of all things belonging to the Company, and that all documents, records, notebooks and tangible articles containing or embodying confidential information, including copies thereof, then in our possession or control, whether prepared by Contractor or others, will be left with the Company. Deliverables shall also included, but not be limited to: (i) all source code; (ii) firmware; (iii) executable code; (iv) assembled code; (v) documentation including design and any modification thereto, (vi) schematics; (vii) prototype and/or production hardware.
perturbation.explanation: The inserted phrase 'retain a copy of' contradicts the initial instruction to 'return to the Company all things belonging to the Company'. This creates an in-text contradiction concerning whether the contractor must return all items or is allowed to keep copies after termination, generating confusion about the obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: D. Termination of Work and Deliverables . Contractor agrees that, upon termination of our contract with the Company (voluntary or otherwise), Contractor will retain a copy of all things belonging to the Company, and that all documents, records, notebooks and tangible articles containing or embodying confidential information, including copies thereof, then in our possession or control, whether prepared by Contractor or others, will be left with the Company. Deliverables shall also included, but not be limited to: (i) all source code; (ii) firmware; (iii) executable code; (iv) assembled code; (v) documentation including design and any modification thereto, (vi) schematics; (vii) prototype and/or production hardware.
perturbation.justification: The changed text instructs the Contractor to 'retain a copy of all things belonging to the Company', while the contradicted text, located in the same section, states the Contractor agrees to return all things. This creates a direct contradiction because the contractor cannot both 'return all things' and 'retain a copy of all things'. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction, aligning with the definition of inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: A. During The Time Contractor is Working for the Company . Contractor agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not interfere with the business of the Company in any manner. Particularly, but without limitation, Contractor agrees to refrain from planning or organizing a competitive business during the time Contractor is working for the Company. Contractor further agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not have any proprietary interest in any competitive business except for an interest of less than five percent (5%) of the outstanding shares of a publicly-held corporation, meaning a corporation whose outstanding shares are owned by one hundred (100) or more shareholders. Contractor further agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not solicit customers of the Company for any purpose whatsoever. In addition, Contractor represents and warrant that Contractor does not presently perform or intend to perform, during the term of this Agreement, consulting or other services for, or engage in or intend to engage in an employment relationship with, companies and / or individuals who have businesses or proposed businesses in any way involve products or services which would be competitive with the Company’s products or services, or those products or services proposed or in development by the Company during the term of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: A. During The Time Contractor is Working for the Company and for 6 months after. Contractor agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not interfere with the business of the Company in any manner. Particularly, but without limitation, Contractor agrees to refrain from planning or organizing a competitive business during the time Contractor is working for the Company. Contractor further agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not have any proprietary interest in any competitive business except for an interest of less than five percent (5%) of the outstanding shares of a publicly-held corporation, meaning a corporation whose outstanding shares are owned by one hundred (100) or more shareholders. Contractor further agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not solicit customers of the Company for any purpose whatsoever. In addition, Contractor represents and warrant that Contractor does not presently perform or intend to perform, during the term of this Agreement, consulting or other services for, or engage in or intend to engage in an employment relationship with, companies and / or individuals who have businesses or proposed businesses in any way involve products or services which would be competitive with the Company’s products or services, or those products or services proposed or in development by the Company during the term of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'and for 6 months after' to the beginning of the clause, but only changing the first sentence. The subsequent sentences in the paragraph still refer to restrictions applying 'during the time Contractor is working for the Company'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the opening now suggests a 6-month post-employment restriction, while the rest of the paragraph only discusses concurrent employment restrictions. This ambiguity undermines the enforceability of any post-employment restrictions due to the contradictions within the original paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. During The Time Contractor is Working for the Company and for 6 months after. Contractor agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not interfere with the business of the Company in any manner. Particularly, but without limitation, Contractor agrees to refrain from planning or organizing a competitive business during the time Contractor is working for the Company. Contractor further agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not have any proprietary interest in any competitive business except for an interest of less than five percent (5%) of the outstanding shares of a publicly-held corporation, meaning a corporation whose outstanding shares are owned by one hundred (100) or more shareholders. Contractor further agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not solicit customers of the Company for any purpose whatsoever. In addition, Contractor represents and warrant that Contractor does not presently perform or intend to perform, during the term of this Agreement, consulting or other services for, or engage in or intend to engage in an employment relationship with, companies and / or individuals who have businesses or proposed businesses in any way involve products or services which would be competitive with the Company’s products or services, or those products or services proposed or in development by the Company during the term of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the initial timeframe of the restriction to include 'and for 6 months after' the employment period. However, the subsequent sentences in the same paragraph still only reference restrictions 'during the time Contractor is working for the Company.' The contradicted text, being the same paragraph as the changed text, now contains conflicting statements about the duration of the restrictions, creating an in-text contradiction as described in the perturbation explanation.
